In a house of a single hall, the height is equal to its breadth. For,
says Sakra :—
t^^TT^T 1
nfcm:!
According to this the breadth should not exceed the length in
a single-hailed house. However, should it exceed the length, such
houses would be called Utsargas, according to Gautama. Single-hailed
houses arc not consiructed for Brahmanas etc. Such houses are not
conducive to longevity, but, if made of straw, they are good.
